questions.question: What are democratic outcomes?
questions.correct_answer: The results or effects produced by democratic governance.
questions.explanation: Democratic outcomes refer to what democracy achieves for citizens and society.

questions.question: What is meant by an accountable government?
questions.correct_answer: A government that is answerable to citizens for its actions and decisions.
questions.explanation: In a democracy, people can question leaders and demand explanations.

questions.question: What is transparency in government?
questions.correct_answer: The openness of government processes and decisions to public scrutiny.
questions.explanation: Citizens have the right to know how decisions are made.

questions.question: Why is democratic government considered legitimate?
questions.correct_answer: Because it is chosen by the people through elections.
questions.explanation: Legitimacy comes from the participation and support of citizens.

questions.question: Does democracy always guarantee economic growth?
questions.correct_answer: No, economic growth depends on several factors besides the political system.
questions.explanation: Factors like population size, global conditions, and economic policies affect development.

questions.question: What is economic inequality?
questions.correct_answer: Unequal distribution of income and wealth among citizens.
questions.explanation: In many countries, a small group controls a large share of wealth.

questions.question: How does democracy help manage social diversity?
questions.correct_answer: By providing mechanisms for negotiation and peaceful conflict resolution.
questions.explanation: Democracy encourages respect for different groups and viewpoints.

questions.question: What does majority rule mean in a democracy?
questions.correct_answer: Decisions are made according to the preference of the majority while respecting minority rights.
questions.explanation: Majority rule should not suppress minority groups.

questions.question: How does democracy promote dignity among citizens?
questions.correct_answer: By recognizing equality and respecting individual rights.
questions.explanation: Every citizen deserves respect regardless of gender, caste, or religion.
questions.source_section: Dignity and Freedom of the Citizens

questions.question: Why do people continue to support democracy despite its shortcomings?
questions.correct_answer: Because it gives citizens the power to participate and influence government decisions.
questions.explanation: Democracy allows people to correct mistakes and demand better governance.
questions.source_section: Dignity and Freedom of the Citizens

notes.title: Accountable Government
notes.content: In democracy, government decisions must follow rules and procedures, and citizens have the right to question these decisions.

notes.title: Economic Growth
notes.content: Democracy does not guarantee rapid economic growth but generally performs comparably with other political systems.

notes.title: Economic Inequality
notes.content: Many democracies still experience large gaps between the rich and the poor.

notes.title: Social Diversity
notes.content: Democracy helps manage social differences by encouraging negotiation and mutual respect among groups.

notes.title: Dignity and Freedom
notes.content: Democracy promotes respect, equality, and freedom for all individuals in society.
notes.source_section: Dignity and Freedom of the Citizens

facts.fact: Democratic governments may take longer to make decisions because they follow procedures and public debate.
Economic development depends on many factors, not just the type of government.
Many democratic countries still face large economic inequalities.
Democracy helps societies handle social conflicts peacefully through dialogue and participation.
Democracy strengthens the dignity and freedom of individuals by recognizing equal rights.
facts.source_section: Dignity and Freedom of the Citizens
